avoid.market: Fed rate cut cluster (KXRATECUTCOUNT-26DEC31-T0 / KXRATECUT-26DEC31 / KXRATECUTCOUNT-26DEC31-T1)
avoid.reason: Internal cross-market consistency check shows P(0 cuts) + P(>=1 cut) = 0.789 + 0.265 = 1.054, implying only ~5% relative mispricing — below the 8% macro edge threshold. The divergence is further muddied by mismatched resolution windows (T0 counts from Jan 1; KXRATECUT counts only from Feb 26). No independent grounding on 2026 FOMC path available without external data. Skip.
Passes liquidity filters, but no verifiable, current spot/futures reference available to independently price a 1-day oil settlement. Any probability estimate would be fabricated. Skip.
18-month horizon, no credible basis to outperform a liquid market's 41.5% prior on 2027 GDP prints. Capital lockup until 2028 also degrades risk-adjusted return. Skip.

avoid.market: All Brent/WTI daily and monthly strike ladders
avoid.reason: Every contract fails the $50K volume filter except the single WTI weekly; strike ladders are internally consistent (monotonic, well-ordered), offering no arbitrage. No spot reference to ground directional views.
All fail the $50K volume filter. Same-day 5:00 PM close markets require live spot data that is unavailable; no defensible edge.
All fail the $50K volume filter, and no NWS point forecast or hourly obs data available to ground an estimate. Bucket probabilities sum to ~0.94-1.00, showing no structural mispricing.
Fail volume filter; narrow $40 buckets on same/next-day crypto close require live spot and realized-vol data. Crypto threshold of 12% cannot be credibly cleared.

Q2 advance estimate (Jul 30) is the catalyst, but all except the T2.5 contract fail the volume filter (T2.5 itself is $47.1K, marginally below), and without current nowcast data (GDPNow/NY Fed) any deviation from the market-implied ~1.8-2.0% central estimate would be ungrounded.
Fail volume filter; resolution not until Aug 12 release. No current inflation nowcast available to justify deviating from market pricing.
Fail volume filter by wide margin, wide spreads, and season-total outcomes require current NHC/CPHC season data unavailable internally for 2026.

notes: Regime classified as inflationary: markets price ~79% probability of zero Fed cuts in 2026, ~26% chance of YoY CPI above 3.5%, Brent near $84, and gold near $4,040 — consistent with sticky inflation and a hawkish Fed hold. After applying the spread (<=0.10) and volume (>=$50K) filters, only five markets qualify: the three Fed rate-cut contracts, the WTI weekly, and the 2027 recession contract. The rate-cut cluster shows a modest ~5% internal inconsistency (probabilities sum to ~1.054), which is below the 8% macro edge threshold and partially explained by mismatched resolution windows. The remaining qualifiers require live spot prices or nowcast data that cannot be credibly sourced without external retrieval; estimating them would violate the no-fabricated-sources rule. Zero trades is the correct disciplined output for this scan. Re-evaluate around the Q2 GDP advance estimate (2026-07-30) and July CPI release (2026-08-12) if fresh nowcast data becomes available.
